_comment: D191 Group 4 bounded Western Theater strategic readout. This is a War Effort and Theater Map teaching/readout slice, not a battle-build, not a tactical registry change, not an OOB rewrite, and not a bridge lever.

profile.name: Western Theater Strategic Readout
profile.summary: A bounded readout that makes the Western Theater legible as a war-winning river, rail, army, emancipation, and election story while keeping future playable battles locked.
profile.teaching: The Western Theater was not a side stage. Shiloh, Vicksburg, Chickamauga, Chattanooga, Atlanta, Franklin, Nashville, and USCT service belong in one strategic arc, but this slice only reads and teaches that arc; it does not create new fields.
sources: data/shiloh.json, data/vicksburg.json, and data/chickamauga.json current playable Western battle data.
National Park Service, Campaign for Vicksburg, https://www.nps.gov/vick/learn/historyculture/campaign-for-vicksburg.htm
American Battlefield Trust, Battle of Chattanooga, https://www.battlefields.org/learn/civil-war/battles/chattanooga
American Battlefield Trust, Atlanta Campaign Maps, https://www.battlefields.org/learn/maps/atlanta-campaign

currentArc.label: Shiloh
currentArc.summary: The first enormous Western bloodletting teaches surprise, command friction, Johnston's death, Grant's recovery, and the scale shock that followed the Tennessee River campaign.
sources: data/shiloh.json current playable battle data and teaching cards.
American Battlefield Trust, Battle of Shiloh, source trail recorded in data/shiloh.json.

currentArc.label: Vicksburg
currentArc.summary: The river fortress teaches the Mississippi as a strategic artery: campaign maneuver, siege, severed supply, and a Confederacy split along the river.
sources: data/vicksburg.json current playable battle data and siege teaching cards.
National Park Service, Campaign for Vicksburg, https://www.nps.gov/vick/learn/historyculture/campaign-for-vicksburg.htm

currentArc.label: Chickamauga
currentArc.summary: The bloodiest Western battle teaches wooded confusion, Longstreet's breakthrough, Thomas's stand, and a Confederate field victory squandered into Chattanooga.
sources: data/chickamauga.json current playable battle data and teaching cards.
American Battlefield Trust, Battle of Chickamauga source trail recorded in data/chickamauga.json.

strategicHinges.label: River and rail war
strategicHinges.summary: The Western war turns on waterways, railheads, depots, and the ability to move armies through deep logistical space rather than on a single capital corridor.
sources: data/logistics-rail.json Western routes and rail-corridor teaching data.
National Park Service, Campaign for Vicksburg, https://www.nps.gov/vick/learn/historyculture/campaign-for-vicksburg.htm

strategicHinges.label: From Chickamauga to Chattanooga
strategicHinges.summary: Chickamauga's Confederate victory only mattered strategically if converted. Chattanooga reversed the pressure by reopening supply, breaking Missionary Ridge, and reopening the road into Georgia.
sources: data/chickamauga.json teaching cards on Bragg failing to exploit the victory and the Chattanooga sequel.
National Park Service, Missionary Ridge, https://www.nps.gov/chch/learn/historyculture/missionary-ridge.htm
American Battlefield Trust, Battle of Chattanooga, https://www.battlefields.org/learn/civil-war/battles/chattanooga

strategicHinges.label: Atlanta and the 1864 election
strategicHinges.summary: Atlanta was an industrial, administrative, and rail center. Its fall raised Northern morale, struck Confederate capacity, and helped make Lincoln's reelection far more likely.
sources: data/decisions.json Atlanta and 1864 election teaching cards.
American Battlefield Trust, Battle of Atlanta, https://www.battlefields.org/learn/civil-war/battles/atlanta
American Battlefield Trust, Atlanta Campaign Maps, https://www.battlefields.org/learn/maps/atlanta-campaign

strategicHinges.label: Franklin and Nashville collapse
strategicHinges.summary: Hood's Tennessee campaign shows the Army of Tennessee's late-war strain: Franklin's frontal assault bled it badly, and Nashville shattered it as an effective threat.
sources: American Battlefield Trust, Battle of Franklin, https://www.battlefields.org/learn/civil-war/battles/franklin
American Battlefield Trust, Battle of Nashville, https://www.battlefields.org/learn/civil-war/battles/nashville
data/generals.json Hood and Thomas biography/source trails.

futureLocks.label: Chattanooga
futureLocks.summary: Future battle-build candidate only: Cracker Line, Lookout Mountain, Missionary Ridge, Tunnel Hill, and Ringgold Gap are kept out of this slice.
sources: American Battlefield Trust, Battle of Chattanooga, https://www.battlefields.org/learn/civil-war/battles/chattanooga
National Park Service, Missionary Ridge, https://www.nps.gov/chch/learn/historyculture/missionary-ridge.htm

futureLocks.label: Atlanta / March to the Sea
futureLocks.summary: Future battle-build and campaign-treatment candidate only: Atlanta's rail and industrial role, election effect, and the later March remain last-queue work.
sources: American Battlefield Trust, Battle of Atlanta, https://www.battlefields.org/learn/civil-war/battles/atlanta
data/hard-war.json Sherman's March teaching card and source trail.

futureLocks.label: Franklin / Nashville
futureLocks.summary: Future battle-build candidate only: Hood's Tennessee campaign, Franklin's casualties, Thomas's Nashville victory, and Army of Tennessee collapse remain locked.
sources: American Battlefield Trust, Battle of Franklin, https://www.battlefields.org/learn/civil-war/battles/franklin
American Battlefield Trust, Battle of Nashville, https://www.battlefields.org/learn/civil-war/battles/nashville

futureLocks.label: USCT at Nashville
futureLocks.summary: Future USCT playable-battle lane only: Black combat agency at Nashville stays visible as a lock without being pulled into this implementation.
sources: data/codex.json United States Colored Troops and Black combat-agency entries.
data/primary-sources.json Lived slavery and Black combat-agency source lanes.
American Battlefield Trust, Battle of Nashville, https://www.battlefields.org/learn/civil-war/battles/nashville

guardrails.title: Readout, not registry
guardrails.summary: This slice adds no battle JSON, no tactical module, no expected-roster baseline, no OOB, no casualty table, and no side-choice card.
sources: START-HERE.md D190 live-head override assigning Western-theater strategic readouts as non-battle readouts.
V1-CHECKLIST.md Group 4 status and last-battle queue lock.

guardrails.title: Zero bridge contract
guardrails.summary: The Western Theater readout can summarize logistics, morale pressure, and campaign arc, but it cannot buy combat strength or modify the universal battle model.
sources: DECISIONS.md D74 universal combat model.
DECISIONS.md D176 focused-gate and no-fudge requirements.
